# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM role for cross-account CI/CD access with wildcard trust policy

Parameters:
  RoleName:
    Type: String
    Default: CICD-CrossAccount-Role
    Description: Name of the IAM role

  OrganizationAccountPattern:
    Type: String
    Default: 'arn:aws:iam::*:root'
    Description: Wildcard IAM principal pattern for trusted AWS accounts

  ExternalId:
    Type: String
    Default: ''
    Description: Optional external ID for additional trust validation

Conditions:
  HasExternalId: !Not [!Equals [!Ref ExternalId, '']]

Resources:
  CICDCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Ref RoleName
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowCrossAccountAssumeRole
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref OrganizationAccountPattern
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              !If
                - HasExternalId
                - StringEquals:
                    sts:ExternalId: !Ref ExternalId
                - !Ref AWS::NoValue
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CICDCrossAccountAccessPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - sts:AssumeRole
                Resource: '*'

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Description: ARN of the CI/CD cross-account IAM role
    Value: !GetAtt CICDCrossAccountRole.Arn
